Submit the Inventory Reports of relief goods, medicines and medical supplies, and food supplies as of December 31, 2020 including the supporting documents for distribution and usage.

 The Municipality devise plans and policies as to the operations, maintenance and repairs of the Potable Water Project so it can function properly for the years to come in accordance with the estimated useful life of the infrastructure asset.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed
through the (a) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to
periodically coordinate to reconcile records and determine the
existence of stale checks; and (b) Municipal Accountant to take up
the necessary adjusting entries to reverse the original entries made
and cancel the stale checks.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records on
RPT and SET Receivables; (b) Municipal Accountant to issue the
necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct amount of RPT and
SET Receivables in the books; and (c) Municipal Treasurer to
strategize specifically on how to collect from delinquent taxpayers
and, if warranted, enforce the provisions of Sections 254, 258 and
260 of the RA 7160 in the collection of delinquent taxes.

We recommended and the LCE agreed (a) to instruct all AOs
concerned to liquidate their cash advances immediately; (b) through
the Municipal Accountant, to pursue all available actions to enforce
the (i) immediate settlement of outstanding cash advances; (ii)
timely liquidation of cash advances that will be granted in the future;
and (iii) proper recording of transactions based on account descriptions
provided under the Revised Chart of Accounts; and (c) to refrain
from granting additional cash advances to officers and employees
unless the previous cash advances given to them are first settled.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to mandate the (a) Inventory
Committee to complete the conduct physical count of PPEs and
submit the report on the result thereof; (b) Municipal Accountant
and the designated Property Officer to (i) maintain complete and
correct records/reports for PPEs with provided details; and (ii)
reconcile records of PPE both in Accounting and GSO Office; and
(c) Accounting Office to (i) depreciate all the Municipality’s
depreciable assets; (ii) recognize impairment losses, as warranted;
(iii) provide breakdown of the items comprising the beginning
balances of PPE per class; and (iv) draw the necessary adjusting
entries to correct the ₱3.6 million misclassified PPE items.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the Municipal
Accountant to (a) conduct a thorough review and reconciliation of
accounting and project records, in close coordination with the MEO,
to identify the specific composition of the CIP account; (b) establish
and maintain the required SL for each project; and (c) effect the
necessary reclassifications and adjustments accordingly.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the (a) HRMO to
ensure the continuous updating of the employee leave records to
maintain accuracy and compliance with COA Circular No. 2024-
009, dated July 16, 2024; and (b) Accounting Office to record the
provisions on earned leave credits in accordance with the
recognition and measurement principles outlined in the Circular.

We recommended and the LCE agreed, through the Municipal
Accountant, to ensure full compliance with the disclosure
requirements provided under IPSAS 3.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the MPDO to (a)
sustain the generally high rate of implementation of its 20% Annual
Investment Plan; and (b) continue adhering to the provisions of
Section 287 of RA No. 7160 and DILG DBM JMC No. 2017-1 in the
identification and implementation of enduring development projects.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the (a) MDRRMO
to sustain the effective planning, identification, and implementation
of disaster-related PPAs; and (b) Municipal Accountant to consider
the suggested adjusting entries.

We also recommended and the LDRRM Council and Sangguniang
Bayan agreed to coordinate in the reprogramming of the Continuing
Appropriations from MF – CO into DRRM-related PPAs in
compliance with Section 5.1.11 of the COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the (a) Municipal
Administrator to (i) maintain adherence to the terms and conditions
outlined in current and future fund transfers from other NGAs; and
(ii) ensure the full utilization of fund transfers through proper
planning and coordination to maximize their intended benefits and
achieve program objectives effectively; and (b) Municipal
Accountant to (i) ensure the timely and accurate recording of fund
transfers; and (ii) prepare the necessary adjusting entries.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the MPDO and
the Municipal Engineer to (a) sustain the high rate of NTF-ELCAC
project implementation; (b) continue adhering to the guidelines
provided in the Local Budget Circular No. 54 dated February 6,
2024 and Section 29 of the General Provisions of the FY 2024 GAA,
RA No. 11975; (c) post Annex C or status of program/project
implementation in at least three conspicuous public places in the
locality and in the LGU’s website within 20 calendar days from the
end of the quarter; and (d) send a written notice to the Secretary of
DBM, Secretary of DILG, Chairperson of the NTF-ELCAC, coursed
through the NTF-ELCAC Vice-Chairperson and RTF-ELCAC
concerned, Speaker of the House of Representatives, President of the
Senate of the Philippines, and the Chairpersons of the House
Committee on Appropriations and Senate Committee on Finance
upon posting of the reports.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the HRMO to (a)
establish a uniform policy guidelines in the hiring and management
of JO/COS personnel; (b) require that all future JO/COS contracts
explicitly define the specific duties and responsibilities of the
personnel being engaged, consistent with the service requirements of
the hiring office; (c) ensure that accomplishment reports of JOs are
reviewed and validated against their clearly defined duties before
processing wage payments; and (d) prioritize the filling of existing
plantilla vacancies to reduce dependency on JO/COS workers and
avoid potential duplication of functions.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the (a) BPLO to
verify the gross receipts of taxpayers using the best, if not all, of the
options provided by law and regulations; and (b) Municipal
Treasurer to examine and verify the books of accounts and other
pertinent records of business establishments.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the Offices of
Municipal Cooperative Development Officer/I-REAP Head, and
Municipal Agriculturist to collaborate with government agencies
concerned to: (a) follow up their request for funding of the DA
INSPIRE hog fattening project; (b) help the PGs in securing from
DA regular program the replacement for unserviceable or obsolete
farm machinery; and (c) follow up the release of additional ₱1
million trading capital for the Common Service Facilities for Post-
Harvest Operation of Aromatic Rice subproject from the DA.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the GAD Focal
Point System (GFPS) to continue its efforts to (a) improve gender
mainstreaming by complying with the basic requirements of GAD
institutional mechanism and processes; and (b) ensure the complete
implementation of activities indicated in the GPB to address gender-
issues in the Municipality.

We further recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) issue
appropriate policy directive to disseminate and implement the GPB
for the succeeding Fiscal Years, in compliance with Item C.7 of JMC
No. 2013-01, as amended by Item 6.0 of JMC No. 2016-01; and (b)
through the GFPS to ensure that the submitted Annual GAD AR is
accompanied by the necessary reports as required under Item C.8
(5) of the same JMC.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to ensure strict adherence
to BIR regulations concerning the complete and timely remittance of
all taxes withheld.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to ensure full compliance
with R.A. No. 8291 or the GSIS Act of 1997 by (a) continuing to
withhold GSIS premiums and loan amortizations from employees’
compensation and remitting the same together with the
government’s share to the GSIS; (b) ensuring timely and full
remittance of all contributions to the GSIS, including any
unremitted balances from prior years; (c) regularly reconciling
records with GSIS to prevent accumulation of unpaid obligations;
and (d) promptly addressing any variances or delays to avoid
penalties and protect the benefits and contributions of its officials
and employees.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to (a) continue to comply
with requirements of the R.A. No. 9679 or HDMF law of 2009 on the
withholding of contributions and loan amortizations from
employees’ compensation; and (b) ensure its timely and intact
remittance to the HDMF.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to (a) continue to comply
with R.A. No. 7875 as amended by R.A. Nos. 9241 and 10606 or the
National Health Insurance Act of 2013 on the withholding of
contributions from employees’ compensation; and (b) ensure its
timely and intact remittance to the PHIC.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) enforce the settlement
of disallowances from responsible and liable personnel; and (b)
ensure that payments in the future adhered to pertinent rules and
regulations to avoid audit of suspension, disallowance, or charge.

We recommended and Management agreed to institute remedial measures to ensure compliance with the rules and regulations on the preparation of a detailed inventory record and the conduct of the physical count of its supplies and inventories for an accurate and reliable financial reports consistent with IPSAS No. 1.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to make the necessary adjusting entries  to capitalize the borrowing costs to the above mentioned projects through the following proposed journal entries:
Construction in Progress – 			xxx
Prior Period  Adjustment–				xxx
Construction in Progress – 			xxx
Prior Period  Adjustment–			xxx

We reiterated our recommendation and the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer agreed to reconcile their records and prepare the necessary adjusting entry in order to reflect the correct balances of the RPT/SET Receivables and establish the beginning balance of the RPT and SET receivables based on the updated RPTAR/Taxpayer’s index card.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
Make the necessary adjusting entries  to record the above-mentioned expenditures as assets instead of expense, using the following proposed journal entries as applicable:
School Buildings – 					xxx
Prior Year Adjustment –					xxx
School Buildings – 					xxx
Prior Year Adjustment –					xxx
Provide and compute the related depreciation expense of the building; and
Ensure that other properties, not specifically mentioned in this AOM but sharing similar conditions, are also promptly adjusted in the Municipality’s books of accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Exert effort in locating the accountable officers with long overdue cash advances and issue demand letters for its liquidation especially to those who are still connected with the Municipality including the withholding of the accountable officers’ salary until the liquidation of the subject cash advances pursuant to Section 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002; and
Assess the validity and collectability of each account for appropriate remedial action including the initiation of the procedures required under COA Circular 2023-008 for the write-off of the unsettled balances of the deceased accountable officers.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed discontinue the payment of honorarium to the concerned employees of the Cebu Provincial Government stationed at the MDH pending submission and evaluation of the corresponding legal basis.

We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed to conduct a thorough evaluation of the conditions and constraints of each project/program that restricted its timely implementation and to expedite those projects/programs with ongoing implementation. Accordingly, adopt appropriate remedial measures for the proper and speedy disposition of subject funds in accordance with the respective terms and conditions of the source agencies.

We recommended and the Management agreed to henceforth,
Ensure that progress and final payments are accurately evaluated and supported with complete documentation as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-001 by incorporating the required documents in their checklist of requirements;
Review the accuracy of the information and dates posted or reported in the PhilGEPS portal consistent with the effectivity dates as provided for in the NTP; and
Adopt remedial measures to ensure timely inspection of the reported accomplishment reports and compliance to the provisions of the RIRR of RA 9184 and other GPPB issuances.

We recommended and the Management agreed to re-evaluate the propriety of the contract extension granted and accordingly impose the corresponding liquidated damages, if warranted.
Henceforth, the Management agreed to ensure that the grant of contract suspensions and or extensions are in accordance with the RIRR of RA No. 9184 and pertinent GPPB issuances to ensure timely completion of government projects.

We recommended and Management agreed to prepare and submit its “catch-up plan” to accelerate implementation of  the project by addressing delays, identifying bottlenecks, and implementing remedial measures to regain lost time or avail of the remedies provided for in the RIRR of RA 9184 and its corresponding manuals as warranted, as well as the pertinent documents relative to the resolution of the subject case.

We recommended and Management agreed to rectify the deficiencies in the contract and accordingly revisit its rules and documentation on the hiring of its consultants to ensure that the procurement and the corresponding disbursements thereof are in accordance with the RIRR of RA 9184 and other pertinent rules and regulations with the view of ensuring  economy, efficiency and effectiveness in government operations.

We recommended and the Management agreed to submit a written explanation on the noted deficiencies together with its corrective measures to ensure that its GAD programs, projects and activities are efficiently and judiciously consistent with the provisions of R.A. No. 9710 and PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circulars Nos. 2013-a and 2014-1.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to coordinate with the concerned departments and submit the lacking documents of the above-mentioned fund transfers together with the status of implementation and fund utilization report from the respective Barangays for evaluation by the Audit Team.  Henceforth, Management agreed to ensure that subsequent fund transfers is supported with complete documentation to ensure propriety and accountability of government funds.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to:
Submit a written explanation together with its assessment or evaluation on the necessity of these additional AOs for evaluation by the Audit Team;
Direct the concerned AOs to maintain the required cashbook and to properly observe proper segregation of their functions/duties by refraining other function outside their official designations; and
Institute remedial measures to ensure adherence to the requirements of Section 124 of PD No. 1445 and DOF Department Order No. 085-2019.

We recommended and Management agreed to submit the revised Appointment Order/Contract upon renewal of the existing JOs as well as the proposed internal rules and guidelines hiring of JO workers of the Municipality,  for evaluation by the Audit Team.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to conduct reconciliation of the said accounts to reflect the correct account balances and for the concerned departments to remit intact the amounts withheld within the reglementary period to the BIR, GSIS, PAG-IBIG, and PhilHealth to avoid paying the penalties, surcharges, and interests.
We also recommended and the Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO) agreed to institute remedial measures to ensure that all new employees comply with the required documentation procedures on or before their assumption to office or prior to the release of their first salary.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the LDRRM office to coordinate with other departments in the preparation of the LDRRMFIP and ensure that no overlapping of programs with similar nature and purpose and revisit implementing rules and regulation and other relevant laws pertaining to the procedures on the utilization of the LDRRMF to ensure its optimum use.

We recommended and the HRMO agreed to submit its remedial measures already undertaken to fill in the said positions for evaluation by this office.
We also recommended and Management agreed to intensify its recruitment efforts in encouraging internal and external applicants who are qualified to fill up the vacant positions or provide trainings and programs to its existing personnel to be able meet the minimum qualifications set forth by the Civil Service Commission’s (CSC)rules and regulations.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Assessor, Municipal Treasurer and GSO to submit:
A copy of the title of the registered lots and updated status of the unregistered lots; and
The actions already undertaken as well as its remedial measures to prioritize the registration, transfer, and titling of these lots in the name of the Municipal Government of Minglanilla to ensure ownership and to fully protect the interest of the Municipality in compliance with COA Circular No. 92-386.

We recommended and Management agreed to expedite the updating and amendment of the Municipality’s Local Revenue Code in pursuance to Section 191 of R.A. 7160 to provide additional revenue sources to fund the Municipality’s projects and programs for the general welfare of its constituents.
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to henceforth, release the shares of RPT due to the barangays within five days after the end of each quarter pursuant to Section 271 of RA No. 7160.

We recommended and the
LCE agreed to continue
adhering with the terms and
conditions outlined in
current MOA for fund
transfers and in future
similar transactions.

We also advised that the
LCE to cause the return of
the excess project funds to

local fund, respectively.

We recommended and the

(a) sustain the generally high

(b) continue adhering to the
provisions of Section 287 of

We recommended and the

(a) continue to include in the

We recommended and the

Gender and Development

to continue conforming with

processes.
The Management, through the Municipal Accountant, transfer the unexpended/ unobligated balance of the CY 2023 LDRRM-MOOE and QRF at year-end to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts.

The Management submit a written explanation of the following noted deficiencies on the projects under the design and build scheme, for evaluation by the Audit Team:

Applicability of the design and build scheme for the above-cited projects; and
Absence of proof that the required two-step procedure for the detailed evaluation of the design and build proposals was undertaken.

The Management submit the following:

For each project, a copy of the Evaluation Report by the Municipality and its supporting documents establishing the need for time extension including the computation showing in sufficient detail how the number of days of extension was determined; and

For the Supply and Installation of Intelligent Traffic Signal, a copy of the approved revised PERT/CPM Network Diagram reflecting the effect of the subject time extension on the original contract time.

The Municipal Budget Officer and MPDC-Designate ensure that appropriations in the Annual Investment Plan under the 20 per cent Development Fund are specifically defined and include only those PPAs that are well-planned and implementation-ready and institute remedial measures for the timely submission of the detailed proposals from the concerned departments or barangays.

The Municipal Mayor and other concerned officials agreed to conduct a thorough evaluation of the conditions and constraints of each project/program that restricted its timely implementation. Accordingly, adopt appropriate remedial measures for the proper and speedy disposition of subject funds in accordance with the respective terms and conditions of the source agencies.

The Municipal Mayor ensure that the Municipal Accountant submit all DVs for CF cash advance with complete required supporting documents to the Audit Team within seven (7) days from the release of the check pursuant to COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01.

The Municipal Accountant agreed to: (i) record the replenishment of the Petty Cash Fund (PCF) by directly charging to the expense account; and (ii) ensure that the PCF account shall only be used during the establishment of the fund and upon liquidation at year-end.

from the list of the identified creditors to determine which of the payables are still
valid obligation; (2) drop from the books of accounts the undocumented
obligations/payables; and (3) make necessary adjustments in the books of accounts if

of reports that will be useful in the decision-making process of the rightful
authorities.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed that the Local
Chief Executive to direct the Local DRRM Officer and Council to ensure the
preparation and submission of a more complete and comprehensive Local
Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Investment Plan which shall include
under a separate caption and detailing the lists of Projects, Programs and
Activities (PPAs) to be financed under the Special Trust Fund – DRRM.

In view of the above-mentioned observations, we recommended and
Management agreed on the following:
1. For the Local Chief Executive to require the offices/officials concerned to
identify the gaps in the determination and implementation of development
projects thereby come up with corrective measures to ensure optimal
utilization of funds and timely delivery of value-added services and benefits to
constituents;
2. For the Local Development Council and Local Finance Committee to assign
specific timelines for the identified development projects to evaluate
achievement rate and identify areas with major shortfall or needs
improvement in order to make timely and periodic remedial measures and
determine the feasibility of implementation of prior year’s unimplemented
projects; and
3. For the Local Budget Officer to determine the status of excess funds from
completed projects and ensure that the aforementioned funds were free from
any encumbrances and recommend to the Local Chief Executive and
Sanggunian Bayan for its reversion and re-appropriate it to other
development projects of the LGU.

We recommended and reiterated and Management agreed on the following:
a. Direct the Inventory Committee to strategize the conduct of complete
physical inventory taking of all the assets and properties of the LGU and
submit updated RPCPPE to the auditor not later than January 31 of each
year in compliance with the existing rules and regulations1;
b. Direct the Inventory Committee to ensure that the reconciliation process
between the physical inventory records of the MGSO and accounting
records are properly in placed, to come up with a reliable report.

In view of the above-mentioned observations we recommended and Management agreed
for the Local Chief Executive direct the:
The Municipal Accountant to:
a. Regularly prepare the bank reconciliation statement of all depository
accounts and furnish the Municipal Treasurer and COA Auditor a copy with
bank statements, JEV recording reconciling items as well as a copy of the
letter to the respective bank informing them of errors noted in the BRS
(other than those resulting from the timing of recording of transactions);
b. Prepare journal entry voucher for the cancellation and reversion of stale
checks, set up receivable from payor whose check was subsequently
returned and record deposit on letters of credit, financial expenses and
other valid reconciling items;
The Acting Municipal Treasurer to:
c. Refund the amount of P3,200.00 for the bank service charge incurred due
to insufficient funds. Hence monitor regularly the cash balances and close
depository accounts in which the purpose had already been satisfied to
avoid possible dormancy charges or below maintaining balance bank
charges.

In view of the above-mentioned observations we recommended and
Management agreed to improve the present monitoring and control measures on the
liquidation of fund releases. Henceforth, additional fund transfer should only be
granted when the previous ones are settled or liquidated.

We recommended and Management agreed on the following for the Local Chief Executive
to:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to conduct regular monitoring and
analysis of receivable accounts to ensure that these are collected when
these become due and demandable and that cash advances and fund
transfers are liquidated within the prescribed period depending upon their
nature and purpose;
b. Continue exerting efforts to collect the overdue receivables;
c. File request for authority to write-off dormant accounts pursuant to COA
Circular No. 2016-005 and direct Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to
gather documents in support of the request.

In line with the foregoing observations, we recommended and Management
agreed to abide with the provisions of COA Circular 94-013 by returning the unused
or excess funds to the Source Agency upon completion of the project.

In view of the above-mentioned observations, we recommended and Management agreed
for the Local Chief Executive to:
a. Direct the MEO to exert effort in tracing the program of works (POW) for
the P3.65 million construction of line canal at Barangay Poblacion. In case
of failure to locate the records, evaluate the necessity and propriety of the
project and consider reprogramming the idle funds to other LDRRM
priority PPAs;
b. Henceforth, establish proper monitoring of all projects being implemented
by the LGU and ensure that no projects are being overlooked;
c. Direct the LDRRM officer to include savings from completed projects in the
preparation of LDRRMFIP.

In view of the above-mentioned observations we recommended and Management agreed
on the following:
a. Cope up with the prior years’ backlogs and ensure that POW and Detailed
Engineering Estimates are promptly prepared to facilitate implementation
of projects within the targeted completion dates;
b. Conduct comprehensive analysis of funding sources and ensure that PPAs
are properly linked with those sources and service delivery outcomes,
prioritize projects which are ready for implementation for constituents to
enjoy the benefits at the earliest opportunity;
c. Ensure optimal utilization of the 20% LDF pursuant to the intent of DILG-
DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 by prioritizing the PPAs
which are attainable.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:
Submit a copy of the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Accounting Office for reconciliation with the ledger cards, and furnish the same Report to the Office of the Auditor;
Maintain and update property records for periodical reconciliation with the Accounting records; and
Make appropriate and necessary adjustments in the books of accounts.

We recommended the following: 
Revisit the Republic Act 9184 provisions, particularly for those bidders who were not able to comply with the requirements specified under the Procurement Law to avoid delays in the prosecution of infrastructure projects; and
Comply promptly all the requirements by the National Government Agencies providing counterpart funding for projects to be implemented in the Municipality.

We recommended that all program and activities that are to be implemented by the LGU should contain specific as to the amount, name, nature and place to be undertaken.  Henceforth, lump sum appropriation should be avoided.  Instead, ensure that the local budget operationalizes the approved local development plans and contains identified objects of expenditures for each function, activity and project.
We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office establish and maintain a special account for the transactions under the 20% Local Development Fund.

We recommended that the BAC prepare and submit the PMR to the GPPB.  Aside from compliance with rules and regulations in the submission of the PMR, the Local Chief Executive, through the BAC, should make use of this report as a tool in monitoring, assessing and improving the procurement process of the Municipality.

We recommended that the twenty four (24) personnel who participated to the regional assembly and sportsfest be required to refund the excess amount of ₱49,600.00 representing the excess amount of the registration fees over the prescribed rate.

If found to be uncollectible, consider the filing of request for authority to write off the accounts from the Commission on Audit pursuant to Section 4 of Rule VI of the 2009 Revised Rules and Procedures.
